rulings.Case Title: IN RE: GEORGE LOEW ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 6, 2021 line 1. PETITIONER GEORGE LOEW, JOSEPH MALLON, ANDREW LUSARDI, JANE FRESTON, FLORENCE SURRATT, MARTIN MILLER Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration Status Report. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

PLAINTIFF THREE EMBARCADERO CENTER VENTURE, A CALIFORNIA Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses Of Defendant And Cross Complainant The Gap Inc GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. rulings.Case Title: THREE EMBARCADERO CENTER VENTURE, A CALIFORNIA VS. THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Produce Documents And Provide Further Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 6, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Produce Documents And Provide Further Discovery Responses GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED in full as to RFPs 2, 3, 5, 18, 37, 38, 45, 51, 55, 60, 63, and 64; Interrogatories 6. Motion is GRANTED in part as to RFP Nos. 7, 8, 9, 10, 13, 15, 42, 73, 74, 75, and 76 as outlined in the Report and Recommendation. Motion is DENIED as to Interrogatory Nos. 15, 17, and 18, and as to RFP Nos. 11, 17, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 32, 33, 34, 39, 40, 41, 43, 46, 47, 49, 52, 53, 54, 56, 57, 58, 59, 61, 62, 65, 66, 67, and 68. Request for sanctions is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
